literal: truth-bearing; *ṛtam* 'the real, cosmic order, truth' + *-bharā* 'bearing, holding' (√bhṛ 'to bear'), a feminine compound qualifying *prajñā* — 'the insight that bears (only) the truth'
invariant_definition: *Ṛtambharā* is, in Pātañjala Yoga, the epithet of the insight (*prajñā*) that arises at the height of absorption: 'there [in that absorption] the insight is truth-bearing' (*ṛtambharā tatra prajñā*, YS 1.48). The insight so named carries only the real: free of error and verbal-conceptual overlay, it grasps its object as it is, with a content distinct from that of inference and testimony because it bears upon the particular object directly (YS 1.49). It belongs to the upper reaches of *samprajñāta-samādhi*, where reflection (*vicāra*) has become clear (*vaiśāradya*) and the mind settled (*adhyātma-prasāda*, YS 1.47); the impression (*saṃskāra*) it generates is held to obstruct other impressions, clearing the way toward *asamprajñāta* absorption and *kaivalya* (YS 1.50–51). The term is specific to the Yoga system, but it is frequently cited in discussions of *prajñā* across the tradition as the type-case of a cognition that is wisdom in the strict sense — discerning insight into the real, not the accumulation of information.

school_framing.shared_core: *Ṛtambharā* names **the truth-bearing insight that arises at the height of absorption and grasps its object as it is, free of error and verbal overlay** (YS 1.48–49). The term is proprietary to Pātañjala Yoga; its wider use is as a paradigm of *prajñā* as discerning insight into the real.
school_framing.register_axes_note: Not a contested term but a single-system technical epithet of *prajñā*. Cited comparatively to illustrate the **shared** structural role of *prajñā* as culminating, truth-disclosing wisdom, even though the object disclosed differs by school.

per_school.definition: *Ṛtambharā prajñā* is the truth-bearing insight that dawns when, in absorption, reflection has grown lucid and the mind is settled (YS 1.47–48). Its content is held to be of a different order from that of inference and verbal testimony, since it bears directly on the particular as it is (YS 1.49); the *saṃskāra* it leaves overrides other impressions, and when even this insight-impression is stilled, *nirbīja-samādhi* and *kaivalya* follow (YS 1.50–51). It is wisdom proper — the immediate discernment of the real that the whole discipline of absorption is meant to ripen.
primary_loci: Yoga-Sūtra 1.49 (its content distinct from inference and testimony)

translator_note: Render *ṛtambharā* as 'truth-bearing' (insight) rather than the vaguer 'filled with truth'; the force is that this *prajñā* carries only the real, no error. Do not flatten *prajñā* here into 'thought' or 'idea' — at YS 1.48–49 it is precisely the discerning insight whose content differs in kind from inference and testimony because it grasps the particular directly. The term is Yoga-specific; when cited elsewhere it functions as a paradigm of wisdom-as-realization.
